timeline.label: Q1 2026 sales $8.78B (+8% YoY) and diluted EPS $3.98 (+13%); operating profit $2.44B (+12%)
FY2025 10-K: revenue $34.0B (+3%), operating income $8.92B, free cash flow ~$5.1B; $4.6B share repurchases and $2.8B dividends
FY2025 earnings release: adjusted EPS $16.35 (+7%); raised quarterly dividend 10% to $1.65 per share
Board authorized additional share repurchase program; FY2025 repurchased $4.6B of stock
Issued long-term debt to fund capital return; net long-term debt rose to $20.7B by year-end 2025
Expanded clean hydrogen and electronics specialty gases project pipeline in APAC and Americas
Completed tuck-in acquisitions (~$412M cash in FY2025) to extend on-site density
Sanjiv Lamba CEO comp and performance metrics tied to ROIC and adjusted EPS growth (DEF 14A 2024)
Sanjiv Lamba succeeded Steve Angel as CEO after Praxair-Linde integration phase
Closed Praxair-Linde merger creating world's largest industrial gases company (~$30B pro forma sales)

industry.structure: Global industrial gases is a tight oligopoly (Linde, Air Liquide, Air Products) with high entry barriers from on-site plant capital, long take-or-pay contracts, and local density in merchant bulk and cylinder networks.
industry.share_shift: Linde holds the number-one global share post-2018 merger; gaining in electronics specialty gases and hydrogen while merchant volumes track industrial production.
industry.trend: Secular demand from semiconductors, healthcare, and clean hydrogen; energy-cost pass-through and pricing discipline offset cyclical industrial volumes.
